title: Gated synthesis flow + engine wiring
description: Compose the grounding critic with synthesis into a fail-closed gate, and wire it into the engine's grounded-synthesis branch. Add synthesizeGrounded (new export in src/medical/retrieval/literature.ts, or a sibling grounded-gate.ts) that: (1) runs synthesize (literature.ts:98) to get the candidate MedicalAnswer; (2) if the candidate is already abstained/short-circuit, returns it unchanged; (3) otherwise runs getGroundingVerdict on the candidate body + its passages; (4) on approve -> return the candidate; (5) on reject -> re-run synthesize ONCE more, appending the critic feedback to the synthesis system prompt, then re-critique; (6) on second approve -> return the re-synthesized answer; on second reject OR any thrown transport/model error at any step -> return an ABSTAINED MedicalAnswer (abstained:true, citations:[], footer present, body = the canned 'cannot ground a sufficiently-supported answer' message). The whole flow is wrapped so a thrown error from synthesize or the critic maps to abstain (fail-closed). Replace the single synthesize call at src/medical/engine.ts:402-403 with synthesizeGrounded, threading the same llmClient, footer, and the grounded RetrievalOutcome. Preserve EVERY existing zero-LLM negative assertion: consent-refuse, red-flag short-circuit, content-policy refuse, numeric-only, and literature-disabled paths must construct NO grounding critic and make NO critic LLM call. Bound the grounded-path LLM calls by a named constant GROUNDED_GATE_MAX_LLM_CALLS (synthesize + critic + at most one re-synth + one re-critique).

successCriteria.description: The project builds with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build) and zero typecheck errors in strict mode (npm run typecheck).
The full test suite passes with zero failures including new collocated tests for synthesizeGrounded and updated src/medical/engine.test.ts (npm run test); no regression in the pre-existing suite.
When the critic approves the first synthesis, synthesizeGrounded returns the original grounded MedicalAnswer unchanged with its >=1 citations intact (asserted on a fake whose critic approves).

When the critic rejects then approves the re-synthesis, the RE-synthesized grounded answer is returned (distinct body, still >=1 citation); when it rejects BOTH, the result is abstained:true with citations:[] and the disclaimer footer present.

Any thrown error from synthesize or the critic (transport/model-unavailable) results in an abstained MedicalAnswer (abstained:true, citations:[]), never a propagated exception and never an ungrounded answer — asserted with fakes that throw at each step.

On the consent-refuse, red-flag short-circuit, content-policy refuse, numeric-only (sampleCount>0), and literature-disabled paths, MedicalSopEngine.run makes ZERO grounding-critic LLM calls — asserted with an injected spy LLMClient that fails if called, exactly as the existing red-flag spy assertion (engine.ts:53-56). Existing engine.test.ts zero-LLM assertions still pass.
The grounded path issues at most GROUNDED_GATE_MAX_LLM_CALLS LLM calls; a call-counting fake on a reject->reject scenario asserts the cap (synthesize + critic + re-synth + re-critique).

No new SDK/network import is added under src/medical; the scoped medical ESLint boundary passes (npm run lint). synthesizeGrounded depends only on the injected LLMClient and the Sprint-1 grounding-critic module.

nonGoals: Do not add config.medical model/provider fields or EgressGuard cloud gating (Sprint 3).
Do not add the AuditEntry.criticVerdict field or change audit calls (Sprint 3) — the engine still appends the existing answer/abstain event this sprint.
Do not change the MedicalSopEngine.run signature, the zero-arg constructor, or any upstream gate (consent/red-flag/refuse/numerics/factstore/egress).
Do not add more than ONE re-synthesis round.
Do not introduce a cloud client — keep using the existing local default client constructed at engine.ts:402.
stopConditions: The grounded branch is gated by the fail-closed critic with a single bounded re-synthesis, every non-grounded path remains zero-LLM, and build/typecheck/test/lint are green with no regression
definitionOfDone: synthesizeGrounded (in src/medical/retrieval/literature.ts or a sibling grounded-gate.ts) composes synthesize + getGroundingVerdict with one bounded re-synthesis and fail-closed abstain on second-reject or any thrown error, bounded by GROUNDED_GATE_MAX_LLM_CALLS. src/medical/engine.ts grounded branch (engine.ts:396-403) calls synthesizeGrounded instead of synthesize, threading the existing llmClient + footer. All existing engine negative assertions (zero LLM on consent/red-flag/refuse/numeric/disabled) still hold. Collocated tests cover approve, reject->approve, reject->reject->abstain, throw->abstain, the call cap, and the zero-LLM non-grounded paths. Build, typecheck, test, lint pass with no regression.
assumptions: synthesize remains the single-call grounding primitive (literature.ts:98-177); synthesizeGrounded calls it rather than replacing it, so the abstain-on-disabled/empty/ABSTAIN semantics (literature.ts:104-166) are reused.
The grounded RetrievalOutcome carries outcome.passages (RetrievalOutcome 'grounded' variant); synthesizeGrounded forwards those passages to getGroundingVerdict.
Re-synthesis appends critic feedback to the synthesis system prompt (extra instruction line) without otherwise changing buildSynthesisSystem; the second synthesize still pins to the same passages.
The engine already constructs the local LLMClient lazily only on the grounded branch (engine.ts:400-402); that construction stays unchanged this sprint and is passed into synthesizeGrounded.
The engine still appends event:'answer' or 'abstain' (engine.ts:417) based on the returned MedicalAnswer.abstained; the criticVerdict audit field is deferred to Sprint 3.
evaluatorNotes: sc-2-3: fake critic approves -> assert returned answer === first synthesis (same body, citations.length>=1). sc-2-4: queue reject then approve and assert the SECOND synthesis body is returned; queue reject then reject and assert abstained===true, citations===[], disclaimerFooter present. sc-2-5: make synthesize throw (fake llm.chat rejects) and assert abstained answer (no exception escapes); repeat with critic throwing. sc-2-6: drive MedicalSopEngine.run for each non-grounded path (no consent; red-flag prompt; refuse prompt; numeric prompt with seeded healthStore sampleCount>0; literature-disabled egress) with deps.llmClient = a spy that throws if called; assert run resolves and the spy recorded 0 calls; confirm the pre-existing engine.test.ts zero-LLM cases still pass. sc-2-7: reject->reject path with a counting fake -> assert chat calls === GROUNDED_GATE_MAX_LLM_CALLS. sc-2-8: npm run lint clean; grep the diff under src/medical for new '@anthropic-ai/sdk','openai','node:net','node:http','fetch(' — expect none. Build+typecheck clean; FULL suite no regression.
